immutable_core.semantic_definition: A time-stamped mean arterial pressure measurement event representing event-level ICU MAP rather than a baseline, summary, or stay-level aggregate. Direct MAP is preferred, and same-time SBP/DBP-derived MAP is allowed only when direct MAP is absent under the approved build rule.
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_map must preserve this event-level mean arterial pressure meaning, including the explicit rule that direct MAP is primary and same-time SBP/DBP derivation is fallback only.

canonical_representation.range_role_note: This is the current cleaned retained-output range for the governed MIMIC build, not a claim that every raw source row in every database is already valid.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.note: A later review may lock a blood-pressure semantic anchor after the cross-database event boundary is frozen more formally.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_map from event-level mean arterial pressure to baseline mean arterial pressure
changing std_map from direct-first MAP with same-time SBP/DBP fallback to a different perfusion or hemodynamic score
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same event-level MAP meaning and fallback rule
locking a reviewed external semantic anchor without changing the variable meaning

allowed_database_specific_variation: source code systems and source codes
database-specific timing anchors and local structural keys
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing baseline or stay-level averaged mean arterial pressure as std_map
publishing pulse pressure or other hemodynamic derivatives as std_map

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_map MVP execution should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_map outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
